topic.what: structural drift detection in malware classification using decision tree ruleset analysis
topic.how: comparing extracted rule representations across temporal windows via feature importance metrics prediction agreement activation stability and coverage
topic.why_matters: malware evolution degrades classifier accuracy; reliable drift detection critical for endpoint protection and SOCs to prevent exploitable blind spots

characteristics.what_it_measures: Spearman correlation between structural drift metrics and subsequent accuracy degradation
characteristics.vs_baseline: vs random chance (0) and clustering methods (0.038-0.087)
characteristics.evidence: Table 5: decision tree FeaturePearsonCorrelation mean rho=0.419 std=0.262; all family pairs positive at lag k=1
characteristics.context: FvF 2-month fixed windowing optimal configuration; stronger than RIPPER (0.329) and Transcendent ICE (0.230)

characteristics.evidence: Section 5.2.1: accuracy stabilized around 98% for xmrig berbew expiro cosmu
characteristics.context: FvB setting with 2-month windows and periodic retraining

characteristics.what_it_measures: dimensionality reduction from full EMBER feature set
characteristics.evidence: Section 4.1: SelectKBest reduced to 10 features; accuracy plateaued at 10 features
characteristics.context: 10 selected features from entropy import and section characteristic categories
